entries.name: heat-flux-vector
entries.quantity: Instantaneous heat current times volume, J*V = sum_i e_i v_i - sum_i S_i . v_i (extensive; the 1/V normalization is explicitly NOT applied)
entries.units: energy*velocity, unit-style dependent: eV*Angstrom/ps (metal), kcal/mol*Angstrom/fs (real), epsilon*sigma/tau (lj). Divide by group volume to get a true flux (energy/area/time).
indices: cartesian alpha in {x,y,z}; vector components 1-3 = total J*V, 4-6 = convective part only

artifact.name: c_ID[1..6] (global vector, length 6)
artifact.format_notes: Accessed as c_ID[k] in variables/thermo_style custom, or routed to a file via fix ave/time or fix ave/correlate. extvector=1 (extensive).

source_anchors.what: definition comment: J = jc + jv; jc = sum (ke_i+pe_i) v_i; jv = sum stress_i . v_i; 'normalization by volume is not included'
jv -= stress[i].v[i] using 6-component symmetric stress (minus sign compensates stress = -virial convention)
jv /= nktv2p converts stress*volume (pressure*volume units) back to energy units
output layout: data[6] = {Jx,Jy,Jz, Jc_x,Jc_y,Jc_z} MPI_Allreduce'd
9-component branch when the stress input is compute centroid/stress/atom (pressatomflag==2)
J formula (two-body case) with 1/V prefactor that the compute itself omits
'vector values will be in energy*velocity units'

operation.closed_form: J*V = sum_i (ke_i + pe_i) v_i - sum_i S_i . v_i, with S_i the per-atom stress*volume tensor (S = -virial - ke term), so -S.v = +(virial).v
operation.notes: Consumes three upstream per-atom computes declared by ID: ke/atom, pe/atom (or any peatomflag compute), stress/atom or centroid/stress/atom. Style checks at compute_heat_flux.cpp:49-64.

maps_to.notes: Overlaps LAMMPS_HEAT_CURRENT in omai/thermal_transport/representation/lammps.py. Extends it with: (1) output is J*V (extensive), not J - the volume division is user-side; (2) 6-vector layout with convective-only components 4-6; (3) the nktv2p sign/unit mechanics; (4) the centroid/stress/atom variant required for bonded/many-body contributions.

conventions: Output is J*V, not J: kappa_GK = 1/(V kB T^2) * integral <(JV)_x(0)(JV)_x(t)> dt when built from raw c_flux products (equivalently V/(kB T^2) with J = c_flux/V).
Sign chain: compute stress/atom returns S = -(virial+ke) in pressure*volume units; heat/flux applies jv -= S.v then jv /= nktv2p, netting jv = +virial.v in energy*velocity units.
Use 'compute stress/atom NULL virial' (exclude the kinetic term): the default ke-in-stress double-counts kinetic transport against the convective e_i v_i term (doc note, compute_heat_flux.rst:78-87).
For angle/dihedral/improper/constraint contributions plain stress/atom gives unphysical J; use compute centroid/stress/atom (doc warning, compute_heat_flux.rst:89-97; Surblys 2019/2021, Boone).
fix rigid contributions to y/z components via stress/atom are 'highly unphysical' (doc warning, compute_heat_flux.rst:99-104).
Unit-style dependence enters via mvv2e (ke), nktv2p (stress), and the timestep unit; in lj all factors are 1.

entries.name: ke-atom
entries.quantity: Per-atom kinetic energy ke_i = 0.5 m_i v_i^2
entries.units: energy: eV (metal), kcal/mol (real), epsilon (lj); conversion via force->mvv2e (metal 1.0364269e-4, real 48.88821291^2, lj 1.0)

artifact.name: c_ID (per-atom vector)
artifact.format_notes: Per-atom vector; dumped via dump custom c_ID or consumed by compute heat/flux / atom-style variables (e.g. per-atom temperature proxy v_temp = c_ke/1.5 in lj).

source_anchors.what: ke[i] = 0.5*mvv2e*m*v^2 (rmass and per-type mass branches)
metal-units block: boltz=8.617343e-5, mvv2e=1.0364269e-4, nktv2p=1.6021765e6

operation.closed_form: ke_i = 0.5 * mvv2e * m_i * |v_i|^2
operation.notes: Atoms outside the compute group get 0.0 - group masking silently zeroes, it does not omit rows.

maps_to.notes: Intermediate input to HeatCurrent; candidate new node PerAtomKineticEnergy (Trajectory-tier, hidden). Do NOT map to catalog InternalEnergy - that node is per-phonon-mode hbar*omega(1/2+n), a different quantity.

conventions: mvv2e is the unit-style-dependent (mass*velocity^2 -> energy) factor; in lj it is 1.
Uses instantaneous velocities including any streaming/COM motion (no bias removal).

entries.name: pe-atom
entries.quantity: Per-atom potential energy: the pair/bond/angle/dihedral/improper/kspace/fix energy tally partitioned onto atoms
entries.units: energy: eV (metal), kcal/mol (real), epsilon (lj)

artifact.name: c_ID (per-atom vector)
artifact.format_notes: Per-atom vector; consumed by compute heat/flux or dumped. Optional keywords (pair, bond, angle, dihedral, improper, kspace, fix) restrict which tallies are summed.

source_anchors.what: accumulates force->pair->eatom, bond/angle/dihedral/improper eatom, kspace eatom, fix energy_atom into energy[i]
hard error if per-atom energy was not tallied on the needed timestep (eflag_atom guard)

operation.notes: The partition of many-body/bonded energies onto atoms is the force style's internal convention (typically equal split among participants); only the group sum is convention-free.

maps_to.notes: Intermediate input to HeatCurrent; candidate new node PerAtomPotentialEnergy (hidden). The per-atom decomposition is itself a gauge choice - different equally-valid partitions leave sums invariant.

conventions: Selective term keywords must match those passed to the companion stress compute or the heat flux mixes inconsistent decompositions (compute_heat_flux.rst:78-87).
Ghost-atom contributions reverse-communicated when newton is on; group masking zeroes after communication.

entries.name: stress-atom
entries.quantity: Per-atom stress*volume tensor: S_i = -(kinetic mv v + virial contribution)_i converted to pressure*volume units; the negative of the per-atom pressure tensor, un-divided by any per-atom volume
entries.units: pressure*volume, unit-style dependent: bar*Angstrom^3 (metal), atm*Angstrom^3 (real), epsilon i.e. dimensionless P*V (lj). Conversion factor nktv2p: metal 1.6021765e6 (eV/A^3 -> bar), real 68568.415, lj 1.
indices: Voigt-6 column order: xx, yy, zz, xy, xz, yz

artifact.name: c_ID[1..6] (per-atom array, 6 columns)
artifact.format_notes: Per-atom array; consumed by compute heat/flux, chunk-averaged pressure profiles, or dumped. First arg is a temperature compute ID for kinetic bias removal, or NULL.

source_anchors.what: final scaling 'nktv2p = -force->nktv2p': sign flip (stress = -pressure tensor) plus energy -> pressure*volume conversion
kinetic term mvv2e * m v_a v_b added when keflag (default on; 'virial' keyword turns it off)
'virial' keyword = pair+bond+angle+dihedral+improper+kspace+fix, NO ke term
'per-atom stress is the negative of the per-atom pressure tensor... really a stress*volume formulation... units of pressure*volume'
sum of diagonal over atoms / (d V) = -P consistency statement
column ordering xx yy zz xy xz yz (9-col centroid ordering appended: yx zx zy)

operation.kind: pointwise per-atom evaluation (virial tally gather + kinetic term)
operation.closed_form: S_i^{ab} = -nktv2p * [ mvv2e m_i v_i^a v_i^b (optional) + W_i^{ab} ] where W_i is the per-atom virial tally
operation.notes: Requires vflag_atom tallying on the invoked step (compute_stress_atom.cpp:144-145). Ghost contributions reverse-communicated.

maps_to.notes: Candidate new node PerAtomStress (materials-domain overlap; hidden/Trajectory-tier). This is exactly the object contracted into the virial term of the existing HeatCurrent node. Its group/volume sum grounds the candidate Pressure node (opposite sign).

conventions: SIGN: S = -(pressure tensor); a system under compression has NEGATIVE diagonal S summed over atoms (sum_diag / (dV) = -P).
STRESS*VOLUME: values are stress times (undefined) per-atom volume; divide by e.g. compute voronoi/atom volume to obtain a stress field.
Default includes the kinetic (ideal-gas) term; pass 'virial' for the potential-only tensor required by compute heat/flux for solids.
Kinetic term can use a bias-removing temperature compute (streaming-velocity removal) via the temp-ID argument.
LJ tail corrections (pair_modify tail yes) are NOT in the per-atom stress (doc note, compute_stress_atom.rst:218-223).
Units follow the units style via nktv2p.
example_instances: lammps/examples/KAPPA/in.heatflux:57 (compute myStress all stress/atom NULL virial)

entries.name: centroid-stress-atom
entries.quantity: Per-atom stress*volume tensor with centroid decomposition of bonded/angular/many-body virials - the full asymmetric 9-component tensor
entries.units: pressure*volume (same as stress/atom, unit-style dependent)
indices: 9 columns: xx, yy, zz, xy, xz, yz, yx, zx, zy

artifact.name: c_ID[1..9] (per-atom array, 9 columns)
artifact.format_notes: Drop-in replacement for stress/atom as the stress-ID of compute heat/flux; heat/flux detects it via pressatomflag==2 and uses the asymmetric components.

source_anchors.what: size_peratom_cols = 9; pressatomflag = 2 (distinguishes it from stress/atom's pressatomflag=1)
consumption: component mapping comment rijx*fijx ... rijz*fijy for the 9-component branch
restriction: centroid/stress/atom does not support many-body pair styles (EAM excepted)

operation.notes: Assigns the virial of bonded/angle/dihedral/improper/constraint terms relative to the interaction centroid rather than equal per-atom splits, fixing the heat-flux decomposition for molecular systems (Surblys 2019/2021).

maps_to.notes: Same candidate node as stress-atom (PerAtomStress) with a decomposition scheme value heat_flux_decomposition=centroid; the two differ per-atom (gauge) but sum to the same global virial.

conventions: 9-component (asymmetric) tensor; the extra yx, zx, zy columns matter because the centroid virial is not symmetric per atom.
Recommended over stress/atom whenever angles/dihedrals/impropers/rigid constraints contribute to the heat flux.

entries.name: heat-current-acf-ave-correlate
entries.quantity: Time-correlation of global scalars - canonically <(JV)_a(t0) (JV)_b(t0 + k*Nevery*dt)> averaged over time origins t0
entries.units: product of input units; for heat-flux inputs (energy*velocity)^2, unit-style dependent. NOT normalized by C(0).
indices: lag index k = 0..Nrepeat-1 (TimeDelta column = k*Nevery, in TIMESTEPS not time)
pair index (type auto: diagonal aa only; upper/lower/full: cross pairs)

artifact.name: user-named (e.g. profile.heatflux, J0Jt.dat) and global array f_ID[i][j]
artifact.format_notes: Per-Nfreq section: header line 'ntimestep nrepeat', then one row per lag: 'Index TimeDelta Ncount C_pair1 C_pair2 ...'. Global array columns: 1=TimeDelta(steps), 2=Ncount, 3..=correlations - so f_JJ[3] is the first correlation pair.

source_anchors.what: accumulate(): corr[k][pair] += cvalues[t-k](i)*cvalues[t](j) over the ring buffer - direct O(Nrepeat) sum per sample
file rows: fprintf(i+1, i*nevery, count[i], prefactor*corr[i][j]/count[i])
npair by type: auto=nvalues, upper/lower=n(n-1)/2, auto/upper|lower=n(n+1)/2, full=n^2
size_array_cols = npair+2 (TimeDelta and Ncount prepended)
type auto/upper/lower/auto-upper/auto-lower/full/first semantics; ave one vs running

operation.closed_form: C_ij(k*Nevery*dt) = prefactor * (1/Ncount_k) sum_{origins} V_i(t) V_j(t + k*Nevery*dt)
operation.notes: 'ave one' zeroes accumulation every Nfreq (window estimate); 'ave running' never zeroes (cumulative estimate, used by the canonical GK script). Sampling every Nevery steps; max lag (Nrepeat-1)*Nevery steps.

maps_to.notes: Overlaps LAMMPS_HEAT_CURRENT_ACF in lammps.py. Extends it with the concrete artifact format (lag axis in timesteps, Ncount column, npair+2 array layout), the prefactor keyword, and ave one/running semantics. Existing spec's 'type full for the alpha-beta tensor' is right; note type auto gives only the 3 diagonal components used for isotropic kappa.

conventions: Lag axis is in TIMESTEPS (i*Nevery); multiply by dt for time - dt is unit-style dependent (default 0.005 tau lj, 1 fs real, 0.001 ps metal).
Raw product units - no normalization by C(0) and no volume/kB factors; the GK prefactor is applied downstream.
Direct-sum estimator, not FFT; numerically equivalent to Wiener-Khinchin post-processing of a dumped J(t).
Correlation values are averages over Ncount origins; with ave running the early lags accumulate more origins than a naive window count.
example_instances: lammps/examples/KAPPA/in.heatflux:64-66 (fix JJ all ave/correlate $s $p $d c_flux[1] c_flux[2] c_flux[3] type auto file profile.heatflux ave running)

entries.name: fix-ave-time-series
entries.quantity: Time-averaged global scalars/vectors (generic averaging + file-writing machinery; the artifact carrier for VACF, MSD, RDF, heat-flux time series)
entries.units: inherits the units of its inputs
indices: input value index; (vector mode) row index

artifact.name: user-named text file and global scalar/vector/array f_ID
artifact.format_notes: mode scalar: '# TimeStep <labels>' header then 'step v1 v2 ...' every Nfreq. mode vector: '# TimeStep Number-of-rows' + '# Row <labels>' then a row block per Nfreq. Averages Nrepeat samples taken every Nevery steps.

source_anchors.what: scalar-mode header '# TimeStep <values>'; vector-mode '# TimeStep Number-of-rows' + '# Row ...'
scalar-mode data rows: ntimestep then vector_total[i]/norm

operation.closed_form: <X> = (1/Nrepeat) sum_{m=1..Nrepeat} X(t_Nfreq - (Nrepeat-m)*Nevery)
operation.notes: ave one/running/window variants parallel fix ave/correlate.

maps_to.notes: Deliberately unmapped: pure averaging/IO machinery, not a physical quantity. It realizes the time-average step inside many mapped contractions (VelocityAutocorrelation and MeanSquaredDisplacement accumulation to file, NEMD dT time average).

conventions: Nevery/Nrepeat/Nfreq triplet semantics shared across the ave-family fixes; sampled timesteps must satisfy Nfreq % Nevery == 0 constraints.
Outputs are averages, not instantaneous - mixing f_ID and c_ID of the same quantity in one analysis silently mixes smoothed and raw series.
example_instances: lammps/examples/KAPPA/in.mp:72 (fix ave all ave/time 1 1 1000 v_tdiff ave running)

entries.name: green-kubo-kappa-workflow
entries.quantity: Green-Kubo thermal conductivity kappa_aa = (scale) * trap(C_aa), scale = Nevery*dt/(kB T^2 V) applied to raw (JV)(JV) correlations; isotropic kappa = mean of the three diagonal components
entries.units: native thermal-conductivity units: lj dimensionless (kB=1); metal eV/(ps Angstrom K); real kcal/(mol fs Angstrom K) - conversion to W/mK is an explicit user-side factor (doc example variable 'convert')
entries.program: input-script pattern: variable trap(f_JJ[k])*scale + thermo output (no dedicated compute/fix)

artifact.name: log.lammps thermo columns (v_k11 v_k22 v_k33 v_kappa) + final print
artifact.format_notes: Running kappa printed as thermo custom columns each correlation window; correlation data itself in the fix ave/correlate file. No dedicated kappa output file exists.

source_anchors.what: variable scale equal $s*dt/$t/$t/vol (kB absent because kB=1 in lj) and k11 = trap(f_JJ[3])*scale
trap() special function - trapezoidal-rule sum over a global vector (unit lag spacing)
GK formula kappa = V/(kB T^2) integral <Jx(0)Jx(t)> dt
real-units solid-argon example: explicit convert kcal2J^2/fs/A factor and scale = convert/kB/T^2/V*s*dt

operation.closed_form: kappa_aa = (Nevery*dt/(kB T^2 V)) * trap_k( <(JV)_a(0)(JV)_a(k*Nevery*dt)> )
operation.notes: trap() assumes unit spacing, hence the Nevery*dt factor in scale. V appears once in the denominator because the correlations carry (JV)^2 = J^2 V^2 and the GK prefactor is V/(kB T^2).

maps_to.notes: Overlaps LAMMPS_THERMAL_CONDUCTIVITY_GREEN_KUBO and LAMMPS_CONTRACT_KAPPA_GREEN_KUBO in lammps.py. CORRECTION to the existing spec: the claim 'the time integral to kappa is post-processed (numpy.trapz); LAMMPS does not emit a kappa running average' is too strong - the canonical examples/KAPPA/in.heatflux computes the running kappa NATIVELY in-script via the trap() variable function and prints it in the thermo output/log. There is still no dedicated kappa file (GPUMD-hac.out-style), and the integral has no tau_min/tau_max plateau control beyond the correlation length p.

conventions: kB must be inserted explicitly in non-lj units (boltz in eV/K metal, kcal/mol/K real); the lj example omits it because kB=1.
T in the prefactor is the target/measured temperature - the example uses the set-point t, not the instantaneous temp.
Integration upper limit = correlation length p*s steps; plateau/convergence assessment is the user's job.
Production run under fix nve after NVT equilibration + velocity rescale - thermostats during production would damp the flux correlations.

entries.name: heat-flux-tally-variant
entries.quantity: Heat flux computed by direct pairwise tally during force evaluation (TALLY package) - avoids the per-atom stress decomposition entirely
entries.units: energy*velocity (as heat-flux-vector)

artifact.name: c_ID[1..6] global vector
artifact.format_notes: Note-only entry: pairwise-additive pair styles only; callback-based tally during Pair::compute.

source_anchors.what: same jc + jv decomposition comment, computed via pair callback instead of stress/atom
compute_vector() with did_setup/eflag_global guards

operation.notes: Alternative realization of the same HeatCurrent node; exact for two-body terms, unavailable for bonded/kspace/many-body contributions.

maps_to.notes: Same node as heat-flux-vector; a different decomposition scheme (per-pair tally vs per-atom stress). Cross-checks the stress-based pipeline for pairwise potentials.

conventions: Pairwise interactions only; silently excludes bond/angle/dihedral/kspace terms present in the model.

entries.name: muller-plathe-exchanged-energy
entries.quantity: Cumulative kinetic energy transferred between the cold (bottom) and hot (mid-height) slabs by momentum-conserving velocity swaps - the imposed-heat numerator of Muller-Plathe NEMD kappa
entries.units: energy (extscalar=0 output in energy units: epsilon lj, eV metal, kcal/mol real)

artifact.name: f_ID (global scalar, cumulative since fix creation)
artifact.format_notes: Typically a thermo custom column (E_delta in the example). Reset by re-declaring the fix (in.mp does exactly this between equilibration and production).

source_anchors.what: e_exchange += force->mvv2e * eswap_all (tally of all swaps, converted to energy units)
compute_scalar() returns e_exchange
edim from x/y/z arg; nbin must be even and > 2
swap loop: kinetic-energy exchange via reflection about the pair center-of-mass velocity (handles unequal masses)
slab geometry: lo bin is bottom bin, hi bin just above half height

operation.closed_form: flux = E_exchange / (2 * A * t_elapsed) (factor 2: periodic box carries the flux in both directions)
operation.notes: Every Nevery steps, swaps the Nswap hottest atoms of the cold slab with the coldest atoms of the hot slab; v' = 2 v_cm - v conserves momentum and kinetic energy for unequal masses.

maps_to.notes: Candidate new node ExchangedReservoirEnergy (or ImposedHeat): the measured numerator of ThermalConductivity[transport_model=nemd]. The existing LAMMPS_CONTRACT_KAPPA_NEMD spec consumes it as 'imposed_flux' but the map has no node for the cumulative exchanged energy itself.

conventions: Cumulative since fix (re-)creation - the example re-declares the fix to zero the tally before production.
Divide by 2 for the two periodic flux directions, by cross-section area lx*ly, and by elapsed time to get the flux (examples/KAPPA/README:87-91).
Fix must precede any fix ave/chunk in the input script or profiles are averaged after the swap (init() warning, fix_thermal_conductivity.cpp:111-119).
Kinetic-energy-only exchange: the potential-energy channel equilibrates via ordinary dynamics; imposed flux is exact by construction, the gradient is the measured quantity.

entries.name: fix-heat-imposed-rate
entries.quantity: Imposed heat addition/subtraction rate eflux applied to a group/region by momentum-conserving velocity rescale (two-reservoir NEMD driver)
entries.units: energy/time (argument): eV/ps metal, kcal/mol/fs real, epsilon/tau lj

artifact.name: f_ID (global scalar = most recent velocity RESCALE FACTOR, not energy)
artifact.format_notes: The imposed energy is known analytically as eflux * elapsed_time; the fix's scalar output is the dimensionless rescale factor - a monitoring diagnostic, not the tally.

source_anchors.what: heat = heat_input * nevery * dt * ftm2v; escale = (ke + heat - KE_cm)/(ke - KE_cm); v -> scale*v - vsub (momentum conserved)
compute_scalar() returns the scale factor (average scale for atom-style eflux)
eflux argument documented in energy/time units; can be an equal- or atom-style variable

operation.closed_form: kappa workflow: dQ = eflux * t_run; flux = dQ/(2 A t_run); kappa = flux/(dT/dz)
operation.notes: KE of the thermal (COM-subtracted) motion rescaled so aggregate energy changes by exactly eflux*Nevery*dt each application.

maps_to.notes: Consumed parameter of ThermalConductivity[transport_model=nemd] (the imposed_flux input of the existing contract_kappa_nemd edge). Same candidate node family as muller-plathe-exchanged-energy but specified rather than measured.

conventions: f_ID is a rescale factor, NOT cumulative energy - contrast fix thermal/conductivity and fix langevin tally.
Momentum conserved by subtracting vsub; KE cannot go negative (hard error).
eflux is extensive over the group; a region keyword restricts application spatially (hot/cold slabs).
example_instances: lammps/examples/KAPPA/in.heat:57-58 (fix hot all heat 1 100.0 region hot / fix cold all heat 1 -100.0 region cold)

entries.name: fix-ehex-imposed-rate
entries.quantity: Enhanced heat-exchange (eHEX/a) reservoir: imposed heat rate F with a coordinate correction that reduces the energy-conservation drift of plain HEX by ~1 order of magnitude
entries.units: energy/time (argument), unit-style dependent

artifact.name: (no scalar output; region-scoped velocity rescale + position correction)
artifact.format_notes: Imposed energy known analytically as F * elapsed_time, as for fix heat.

source_anchors.what: rescale(): escale = 1 + (F*dt)/Kr with Kr the reservoir's thermal kinetic energy
'hex' keyword disables the coordinate correction, reducing eHEX to plain HEX

operation.kind: NEMD reservoir driver (rescale + integrator-consistent coordinate correction)
operation.notes: Same kappa post-processing as fix heat; the correction targets the O(dt^3) integration error of the rescale.

maps_to.notes: Same candidate node family (imposed reservoir heat) as fix-heat-imposed-rate; scheme variant with better energy conservation.

conventions: Lives in the RIGID package (supports constrained/SHAKE clusters via the 'constrain' keyword: whole-cluster rescaling by COM membership).

entries.name: langevin-reservoir-energy
entries.quantity: Cumulative energy exchanged between a Langevin thermostat and its group (tally yes) - the measured heat for two-reservoir thermostatted NEMD
entries.units: energy (cumulative), unit-style dependent

artifact.name: f_ID (global scalar, cumulative since fix creation)
artifact.format_notes: Thermo custom column in the example; sign convention: energy added to atoms by the reservoir.

source_anchors.what: end_of_step: energy_onestep = sum flangevin . v; energy += energy_onestep*dt
compute_scalar() returns accumulated reservoir energy (with half-step correction)
tally keyword parsing (requires extra memory; changes setmask to END_OF_STEP)
gamma1 = -m/t_period/ftm2v (drag) - t_period (Tdamp) is in time units, unit-style dependent

operation.closed_form: dQ = average of |E_hot| and |E_cold| tallies; flux = dQ/(2 A t); kappa = flux/(dT/dz)
operation.notes: Langevin force f = -m v/Tdamp + sqrt-term noise; the tally integrates f_langevin . v dt.

maps_to.notes: Same candidate node (ExchangedReservoirEnergy) as the Muller-Plathe tally - measured rather than imposed heat; numerator of ThermalConductivity[transport_model=nemd] for the thermostatted-reservoir variant.

conventions: fix langevin does NOT integrate motion - it must be paired with fix nve (KAPPA example pairs both).
In/out tallies differ slightly; the README averages |hot| and |cold| (README:49-52).
Uniform random noise by default (gjf and other variants change the discretization); seed-dependent trajectory (gauge).
example_instances: lammps/examples/KAPPA/in.langevin:61-62 (fix hot all langevin ${thi} ${thi} 1.0 59804 tally yes)

entries.name: temperature-profile-chunks
entries.quantity: Spatially binned temperature profile T(bin) along the transport axis - the gradient measurement of NEMD kappa
entries.units: temperature: K (metal/real), epsilon/kB reduced (lj). Bin coordinate in reduced (fractional) or box units per the chunk definition.
entries.program: compute chunk/atom bin/1d + fix ave/chunk (values: compute temp/chunk, or an atom-style variable like v_temp = c_ke/1.5)

artifact.name: profile.* (e.g. profile.mp, profile.langevin) and global array f_ID[chunk][col]
artifact.format_notes: Per-Nfreq section: '<timestep> <Nchunks>' then rows 'Chunk Coord1 Ncount value...'; header '# Chunk Coord1 Ncount <label>' (1d binning). Accessed in-script as f_2[chunk][3] for the temperature column.

source_anchors.what: bin/1d style parsing (which = ArgInfo::BIN1D)
output header '# Chunk Coord1 Ncount ...' for 1-coordinate chunks
temp-value normalization: values *= mvv2e/((repeat*cdof + adof*count)*boltz) - per-chunk DOF convention

operation.closed_form: T_chunk = sum_{i in chunk} m_i v_i^2 * mvv2e / (DOF_chunk * kB); the lj examples instead bin the atom variable 2*ke_i/(3 kB) = c_ke/1.5
operation.notes: dT/dz then read as a finite difference of two bins (in.mp: f_2[11][3]-f_2[1][3] across half the box) or a linear fit of the profile file.

maps_to.notes: Grounds the existing Temperature node BEYOND the current 8-variable MD spec: LAMMPS produces T with a spatial chunk index (T(z)), which is what contract_kappa_nemd's 'imposed_gradient' is measured from. The map's Temperature is scalar; the chunked profile is Temperature[bin] - flag for the substrate whether a spatial index is a new node or an index on the existing one.

conventions: Per-chunk DOF: cdof/adof defaults matter (adof defaults to dimension per atom; COM-of-chunk not removed unless temp/chunk com yes); the KAPPA lj scripts bypass this via v_temp = c_ke/1.5 (kB=1, d=3, no COM removal).
Bins declared in reduced coordinates ('units reduced': 0.05 = 20 bins) in the canonical scripts.
fix ave/chunk must come BEFORE fix thermal/conductivity swaps in file order to average pre-swap velocities (warning in fix_thermal_conductivity.cpp:111-119).

entries.name: temp-region
entries.quantity: Instantaneous temperature of atoms currently inside a geometric region (reservoir delta-T measurement for two-reservoir NEMD)
entries.units: temperature: K (metal/real), reduced (lj)

artifact.name: c_ID (global scalar) + c_ID[1..6] (KE tensor vector)
artifact.format_notes: Thermo custom columns c_Thot / c_Tcold in the canonical scripts; dT = c_Thot - c_Tcold.

source_anchors.what: dof = dimension * N_in_region - extra_dof; scalar = mvv2e * sum(m v^2) / (dof * boltz); N recounted every invocation
compute Thot all temp/region hot; compute Tcold all temp/region cold

operation.closed_form: T = mvv2e * sum_{i in region} m_i v_i^2 / ((d*N_region - d) kB)
operation.notes: Region membership is dynamic (recounted per step); no fix_dof adjustments beyond extra_dof.

maps_to.notes: Region-scoped realization of the existing Temperature node; feeds the measured gradient (via slab separation) of ThermalConductivity[transport_model=nemd].

conventions: DOF varies with instantaneous region occupancy; small slabs give noisy T - the profile-based gradient is the lower-variance alternative.

entries.name: nemd-kappa-workflow
entries.quantity: NEMD thermal conductivity kappa = flux / (dT/dz): flux from the reservoir tally (measured or imposed) with the factor-2 periodic correction; gradient from the chunked T profile or region temperatures
entries.units: native: lj dimensionless; metal/real require explicit conversion to W/mK (never performed by LAMMPS)
entries.program: input-script pattern over {fix thermal/conductivity | fix heat | fix ehex | fix langevin tally} + T-profile; kappa assembled via equal-style variables

artifact.name: log.lammps (thermo column / final print of v_kappa)
artifact.format_notes: in.mp computes kappa natively in-script: kappa = (f_3/(time-start)/(lx*ly)/2)*(lz/2)/f_ave. The langevin/heat/ehex variants are assembled from log numbers per the README recipes.

source_anchors.what: in-script kappa variable: swap tally / elapsed time / area / 2, times (lz/2) / time-averaged dT
kappa = dQ * dZ/dTemp recipes for all four NEMD variants, incl. the divide-by-2 periodic-flux rule
overview of the five kappa methods (langevin, heat, ehex, Muller-Plathe, Green-Kubo)

operation.closed_form: kappa = [dQ/(2 A t)] / (dT/dz); Muller-Plathe: dQ = f_ID of the swap tally; two-reservoir: dQ = reservoir energy tally or eflux*t
operation.notes: Gradient from finite difference of two chunk bins (dT over lz/2) or linear fit of the profile. Finite-size scaling (kappa vs 1/Lz) is out of scope of the scripts.

maps_to.notes: Overlaps LAMMPS_THERMAL_CONDUCTIVITY_NEMD and LAMMPS_CONTRACT_KAPPA_NEMD in lammps.py. Two flags: (1) the existing spec's parameter_units {'imposed_flux': 'kcal/(mol*fs)', 'imposed_gradient': 'K/Angstrom'} hard-code REAL units - both are unit-style dependent and the canonical examples are lj; (2) besides Muller-Plathe and 'direct_two_reservoir' (nvt), LAMMPS ships three more reservoir variants (fix heat, fix ehex, fix langevin tally) that the nemd_method scheme should enumerate.

conventions: Factor 2: periodic boxes carry the imposed flux in two directions; forgetting it doubles kappa.
The gradient length is lz/2 (hot and cold slabs half a box apart), hence the (lz/2)/dT form.
The Muller-Plathe dT is larger (1.175) than the langevin/heat variants (0.57-0.77) for the same system - swap-rate choice trades signal against perturbation strength.
All five KAPPA example methods agree within ~15% (3.29-3.88) on the Evans 1986 LJ state point rho*=0.6 T*=1.35.

entries.name: dump-custom-trajectory
entries.quantity: Per-atom trajectory snapshots with user-selected columns - the Trajectory artifact
entries.units: coordinates in distance units (Angstrom metal/real, sigma lj) for x|y|z|xu..., dimensionless fractional for xs|xsu; velocities in velocity units (A/ps metal, A/fs real, sigma/tau lj)
entries.program: dump custom (dump ID group custom N file keywords...)

artifact.name: user-named dump file (text; .gz/.bin variants exist)
artifact.format_notes: Per-frame: 'ITEM: TIMESTEP', 'ITEM: NUMBER OF ATOMS', 'ITEM: BOX BOUNDS <flags>' (3 lines lo hi [tilt]; 'xy xz yz' prefix when triclinic), 'ITEM: ATOMS <columns>' then one row per atom. Rows NOT sorted by id unless dump_modify sort id.

source_anchors.what: frame header writer (ITEM: TIMESTEP / NUMBER OF ATOMS / BOX BOUNDS / ATOMS <columns>)
pack_xu: unwrapped coord = x + ((image & IMGMASK)-IMGMAX)*xprd - unwrapping is exact only while image flags are intact
keyword parse: x/y/z (wrapped, unscaled), xs (wrapped, scaled), xu/yu/zu (unwrapped), xsu, ix/iy/iz (image flags)
xu,yu,zu = unwrapped; xsu,ysu,zsu = scaled unwrapped

operation.notes: Sampling interval N in timesteps. Columns are the exact per-atom state - no averaging.

maps_to.notes: Overlaps LAMMPS_TRAJECTORY in lammps.py. CORRECTION to the existing spec: its note says wrap/unwrap 'is set via dump_modify (use xu yu zu ...)' - xu/yu/zu are dump COLUMN KEYWORDS on the dump command itself, not dump_modify options; and its code_api template 'dump custom <id> all custom ...' duplicates the style token. Extends the spec with the ITEM header grammar and the unsorted-rows default.

conventions: x y z are WRAPPED into the periodic box; MSD/diffusion post-processing needs xu yu zu, or x y z PLUS ix iy iz image flags.
Image-flag unwrapping is wrong across box-size changes (npt) - unwrapped coords from a barostatted run need care.
Default atom order is processor-order, not id-sorted (dump_modify sort id to fix).
BOX BOUNDS header carries boundary style letters (pp, ff, sm...) and tilt factors for triclinic.

entries.name: dump-atom-trajectory
entries.quantity: Default-format trajectory: id, type and SCALED (fractional) wrapped coordinates
entries.units: xs ys zs are dimensionless box fractions (0..1); with dump_modify scale no: distance units; optional ix iy iz image flags (integers)

artifact.name: user-named dump file
artifact.format_notes: Same ITEM header grammar as dump custom; default columns 'id type xs ys zs' (dump_atom.cpp default), '+ ix iy iz' with dump_modify image yes, 'id type x y z' with dump_modify scale no.

source_anchors.what: scale_flag = 1, image_flag = 0 defaults
default column sets: 'id type xs ys zs' / '+ image' / unscaled variants

operation.notes: Fixed column menu; use dump custom for velocities or per-atom compute output.

maps_to.notes: Second realization of Trajectory; the fractional-coordinate default is the trap worth recording.

conventions: DEFAULT COORDINATES ARE FRACTIONAL (xs ys zs), not Angstroms - any consumer treating them as lengths is silently wrong by the box size.
No velocities available in this style.

entries.name: msd
entries.quantity: Mean-squared displacement from a stored reference configuration: 4-vector (dx^2, dy^2, dz^2, total), averaged over group atoms
entries.units: distance^2: Angstrom^2 (metal/real), sigma^2 (lj)
entries.program: compute msd (keywords: com yes/no, average yes/no)

artifact.name: c_ID[1..4] (global vector)
artifact.format_notes: Typically time-series'd via thermo custom, fix ave/time file, or fix vector for slope fitting.

source_anchors.what: reference positions stored UNWRAPPED via domain->unmap at compute creation (fix STORE/ATOM)
current position unwrapped from image flags: x + ((image&IMGMASK)-IMGMAX)*xprd (triclinic h-matrix branch at 219-227)
MPI sum then division by group count (per-atom average)
note: initial coords stored unwrapped using image flags; resettable via set image

operation.closed_form: MSD_k(t) = (1/N) sum_i [r_unwrap,i(t) - r_ref,i]_k^2; component 4 = full |dr|^2
operation.notes: com yes subtracts the group center-of-mass at both reference and current time (drift removal); average yes replaces the fixed reference with a running average position - a different estimator again.

maps_to.notes: Overlaps LAMMPS_MEAN_SQUARED_DISPLACEMENT and LAMMPS_COMPUTE_MSD in lammps.py (whose unwrap_pbc=true override is confirmed by source). EXTENSION the catalog should record: the catalog node is defined as <|r(t+tau)-r(t)|^2> averaged over atoms AND time origins; LAMMPS's compute is a SINGLE-time-origin estimator (reference = compute-creation step). They agree as estimators of 2dDt in the diffusive regime, but are different finite-trajectory quantities - cross-code compare() on raw curves needs matching origin conventions.

conventions: Unwrapping relies on intact image flags: 'set image' resets, box-size changes (npt), or lost atoms corrupt it.
Single fixed time origin - no sliding-window average; re-declare the compute for a new origin.
com yes is required to read self-diffusion cleanly off the slope (COM drift removal).
Group must not change atom count (hard error).
example_instances: lammps/examples/DIFFUSE/in.msd.2d:48 (compute msd all msd com yes)

entries.name: vacf
entries.quantity: Velocity autocorrelation against the stored creation-time velocities: 4-vector (vx.vx0, vy.vy0, vz.vz0, v.v0), averaged over group atoms
entries.units: velocity^2: (Angstrom/ps)^2 metal, (Angstrom/fs)^2 real, (sigma/tau)^2 lj

artifact.name: c_ID[1..4] (global vector)
artifact.format_notes: Time-series'd via fix ave/time (file) or fix vector (for trap() integration).

source_anchors.what: reference velocities stored at compute creation (fix STORE/ATOM)
vacf sums v_i(t)*v_i(0) per component; per-atom average at 123-129

operation.closed_form: C_k(t) = (1/N) sum_i v_{i,k}(t) v_{i,k}(t_create)
operation.notes: Not normalized by C(0). For time-origin-averaged VACF, users either re-create the compute periodically or post-process a velocity dump (or run fix ave/correlate on binned velocity components).

maps_to.notes: Overlaps LAMMPS_VELOCITY_AUTOCORRELATION in lammps.py (which already notes the reset-at-creation behavior). Extension: exact component layout and the absence of C(0) normalization; the Fourier-to-DOS edge stays external (confirms LAMMPS_FOURIER_TO_DOS).

conventions: Single time origin (creation step) - same estimator caveat as compute msd.
No arguments accepted; group defines the atom average.
Thermostats during sampling distort C(t) at times beyond Tdamp - sample under nve.
example_instances: lammps/examples/DIFFUSE/in.vacf.2d:46-52 (compute vacf + fix vector + variable 0.5*dt*trap(f_5) -> D in 2d)

entries.name: rdf
entries.quantity: Radial distribution function g(r) and running coordination number, per requested type-pair, histogrammed in Nbin bins from 0 to the force cutoff (or explicit cutoff)
entries.units: g(r) dimensionless (ideal-gas-normalized); r in distance units; coordination dimensionless

artifact.name: c_ID (global array: Nbin rows x (1 + 2*Npairs) cols: r, then g(r), coord(r) per pair)
artifact.format_notes: Written to file via fix ave/time mode vector; time-averaging over snapshots is the standard usage.

source_anchors.what: gr = hist/(vfrac * normfac * jcount): shell-volume ideal-gas normalization (4pi/3V shell fraction), 2d variant at 418-434
Nbin = arg[3]
bins span 0 to max force cutoff unless the cutoff keyword extends/overrides
default extent = pair-style force cutoff; cutoff keyword requires neighbor-list implications noted

operation.closed_form: g_m(r_b) = hist_m(b) / (Vshell_frac(b) * N_i * N_j / V ... as coded: hist/(vfrac*normfac*count))
operation.notes: Coordination column integrates g * shell density up to r.

maps_to.notes: Candidate new node RadialDistributionFunction (structure-tier observable off Trajectory, sibling of MeanSquaredDisplacement). No existing catalog node covers it.

conventions: Default r-extent is the PAIR CUTOFF, not half the box - RDFs silently truncate at rc (2.5 sigma in the KAPPA systems) unless cutoff keyword is used.
Normalization is ideal-gas at the GLOBAL density of the selected types; inhomogeneous systems (slabs) violate it.
Instantaneous unless routed through fix ave/time.

entries.name: diffusivity-workflows
entries.quantity: Self-diffusion coefficient D via (a) Einstein slope of MSD, or (b) Green-Kubo time integral of the VACF - both realized in-script
entries.units: distance^2/time: Angstrom^2/ps (metal), Angstrom^2/fs (real), sigma^2/tau (lj)
entries.program: input-script pattern: variable slope(f_ID)/(2d*Nevery*dt) on a fix vector of c_msd[4]; or variable (1/d)*trap(f_ID)*dt on a fix vector of c_vacf[4]

artifact.name: log.lammps thermo columns (v_twopoint, v_fitslope, v_vacf)
artifact.format_notes: DIFFUSE examples print both the two-point estimator c_msd[4]/(2d t) and the fitted slope(f)/(2d dt_sample).

source_anchors.what: compute msd com yes; v_twopoint = c_msd[4]/4/(step*dt); v_fitslope = slope(f_9)/4/(10*dt) [2d: 2d=4]
compute vacf; v_vacf = 0.5*dt*trap(f_5) [2d: 1/d = 1/2 with c_vacf[4] the d-summed dot product]
MSD-slope and VACF-integral recipes with slope()/trap() variable functions
trap and slope special functions on global vectors

operation.closed_form: D = slope(MSD_total)/(2 d) = (1/d) integral <v(0).v(t)> dt
operation.notes: d = spatial dimensionality; the factor appears as /4 in the 2d examples.

maps_to.notes: LAMMPS grounds the existing Diffusivity node natively in-script - a node the current lammps.py MD tier does not yet claim (its 8-variable spec stops at MSD/VACF). The single-origin caveats of msd/vacf propagate into D.

conventions: Einstein factor 2d with d the simulation dimensionality (2d examples use 4).
VACF-integral D requires the correlation to have decayed within the run; trap() spacing correction Nevery*dt applies as in the kappa workflow.

entries.name: ensemble-integrator-schemes
entries.quantity: Ensemble/thermostat/barostat declaration - a scheme (gauge) choice on the MD run, not a mapped quantity
entries.units: Tdamp/Pdamp in time units (unit-style dependent: tau lj, fs real, ps metal); target T in temperature units; target P in pressure units
entries.program: fix nve | fix nvt | fix npt | fix nph | fix langevin (composed with fix nve)

artifact.name: (none - modifies the trajectory itself)
artifact.format_notes: Declared as input-script fix lines; thermostat state appears in thermo ecouple/econserve keywords.

source_anchors.what: velocity-Verlet: half-kick v += dtf/m f, drift x += dtv v (default integrator)
FixNH::nve_x position update; nh_v_temp at 2338 = Nose-Hoover half-step velocity scaling
FixNVT is FixNH with tstat only; FixNPT (fix_npt.h:27) adds the barostat
gamma1 = -m/t_period/ftm2v drag + random-force pair; fix langevin does NOT integrate - requires a paired fix nve
tchain keyword - Nose-Hoover chains (default length 3)

operation.notes: One entry on purpose: the ensemble choice fixes which stationary distribution the Trajectory samples and how phase-space is propagated; every per-snapshot quantity (HeatCurrent, per-atom energies) inherits this gauge. Only the time-averaged contractions (ACFs, MSD, profiles, kappa) are the cross-code-comparable content, and even they require the SAME ensemble for compare() (e.g. GK kappa is defined on nve production; thermostatted production damps correlations).

maps_to.notes: Deliberately unmapped - this is the 'ensemble' scheme on the run_md operator (overlaps LAMMPS_RUN_MD OperatorRepresentationSpec in lammps.py). Scheme values observed: nve (velocity-Verlet), nvt (Nose-Hoover chains, tchain=3), npt/nph (+ MTK barostat), langevin+nve (stochastic; seed-dependent), plus temp/rescale and berendsen variants not cataloged here.

conventions: fix langevin and other thermostat-only fixes do not move atoms - forgetting the paired fix nve is a classic silent-wrong-physics input.
Tdamp/Pdamp are in TIME units and thus unit-style dependent (0.5 tau lj vs 100 fs real vs 0.1 ps metal for equivalent damping).
Nose-Hoover uses chains (tchain default 3) - not the single-thermostat textbook form.
KAPPA GK script pattern: nvt equilibrate -> velocity scale -> unfix -> nve produce.

entries.name: pressure
entries.quantity: Global pressure: scalar P = (dof kB T + sum_diag virial)/(d V) * nktv2p and symmetric tensor P_ab = (sum_i m v_a v_b + virial_ab)/V * nktv2p
entries.units: pressure: bar (metal), atm (real), epsilon/sigma^3 (lj), Pa (si)
indices: Voigt-6 tensor (xx,yy,zz,xy,xz,yz) for the vector output; scalar = trace/d
entries.program: compute pressure (auto-instantiated as thermo_press for the thermo 'press' keyword)

artifact.name: thermo columns Press / pxx..pxy in log.lammps; c_ID scalar/vector via API
artifact.format_notes: thermo_style custom press pxx pyy pzz pxy pxz pyz; also consumed by fix npt.

source_anchors.what: scalar: (dof*boltz*T + virial_xx+yy+zz)/3 * inv_volume * nktv2p (2d variant divides by 2)
tensor: (ke_tensor + virial) * inv_volume * nktv2p per component
kspace virial added; pair tail correction ptail added to diagonal
thermo 'press' keyword reads pressure->scalar

operation.closed_form: P = (N_dof kB T + sum_i sum_{j} r_ij . f_ij) / (d V) [in nktv2p pressure units]
operation.notes: Kinetic part comes from the associated temperature compute (bias-removable); potential part from the global virial tally, not from summing stress/atom.

maps_to.notes: Candidate new node Pressure (materials-domain overlap; near-term for the ELASTIC workflow, the npt barostat setpoint, and the enthalpy formula). OPPOSITE sign convention to the per-atom stress: P = -sum(S_i)/(dV).

conventions: Positive-compression sign (physics pressure); compute stress/atom is its NEGATIVE (stress*volume).
Kinetic term uses dof*kB*T of the paired temperature compute, inheriting its DOF/bias conventions.
Includes long-range (kspace) virial and pair_modify tail corrections that the per-atom stress excludes.
nktv2p sets the unit: metal bar, real atm (68568.415), lj 1.

entries.name: elastic-constants-workflow
entries.quantity: Zero-temperature elastic stiffness tensor C_ij (Voigt) by central finite difference of the virial stress under +/- box strain with energy minimization
entries.units: pressure units scaled by cfac to output units: metal bar * 1.0e-4 -> GPa (init.mod); real atm * 1.01325e-4 -> GPa
entries.program: examples/ELASTIC input-script workflow (in.elastic + init.mod + potential.mod + displace.mod); no dedicated compute

artifact.name: log.lammps 'Elastic Constant CXYall = ... GPa' print block
artifact.format_notes: Also derived cubic averages: bulk modulus (C11+2C12)/3, shear moduli C44 and (C11-C12)/2, Poisson ratio.

source_anchors.what: d_i = -(p_i1 - p_i0)/(delta/len0)*cfac - stress-difference over strain, note the MINUS sign from pressure-vs-stress
negative then positive box deformation via change_box ... remap units box, each followed by minimize
cfac = 1.0e-4 (bar -> GPa, metal units); commented alternatives for other unit styles
symmetrization C12all = 0.5*(C12+C21) etc.

operation.kind: finite-difference derivative of stress wrt strain (T=0 statics)
operation.closed_form: C_ij = -dP_i/de_j ~ -(P_i(+delta) - P_i(-delta))/(2 delta) (averaged over both signs in displace.mod)
operation.notes: Atoms re-minimized after each deformation (relaxed-ion C_ij); up = strain magnitude, convergence checked by insensitivity to up. Uses thermo pxx..pxy (compute pressure) as the stress readout.

maps_to.notes: Candidate new materials-domain node ElasticConstants (stiffness tensor). Sits exactly on the announced materials overlap: consumes Potential + CellVolume, produces C_ij; the Si-SW example cross-checks against Cowley 1988 analytical values (C11=151.4, C12=76.4, C44=56.4 GPa).

conventions: T=0 statics (minimize), relaxed-ion; NOT the finite-T stress-fluctuation method.
The minus sign in the formula converts pressure convention to stress convention.
Voigt strain dir 1..6 maps to change_box x/y/z/yz/xz/xy deltas; triclinic tilts require the box to allow tilt.
cfac/cunits are unit-style-specific and live in init.mod - porting to real/lj units means editing them.

entries.name: voronoi-atom-volume
entries.quantity: Per-atom Voronoi cell volume and face count (note-only entry for the materials overlap)
entries.units: volume (distance^3): Angstrom^3 (metal/real), sigma^3 (lj); face count dimensionless
indices: column 1 = volume, column 2 = number of faces/neighbors

artifact.name: c_ID[1..2] per-atom array (more columns with surface/edge/occupation options)
artifact.format_notes: occupation mode instead reports sites-per-original-cell (defect detection).

source_anchors.what: voro[i][0] = c.volume() (Voro++ cell volume); voro[i][1] = face count above fthresh at 513-515

operation.notes: Sum of column 1 over all atoms = box volume (tessellation identity).

maps_to.notes: Note-only. Relevant to the map as the canonical per-atom volume for converting stress*volume (compute stress/atom) into an actual per-atom stress field, per compute_stress_atom.rst:199-200. Not proposed as a standalone node yet.

conventions: Radical/radius-weighted variants and group restriction change the tessellation; sum-to-box-volume only holds for group all without surface options.

entries.name: cna-atom-structure
entries.quantity: Common-neighbor-analysis lattice-structure label per atom: 1=fcc 2=hcp 3=bcc 4=icosahedral 5=other (0=unknown outside group) (note-only)
entries.units: dimensionless categorical label

artifact.name: c_ID per-atom vector (float-encoded category)
artifact.format_notes: Usually dumped for visualization (OVITO-style structure coloring).

source_anchors.what: enum {UNKNOWN, FCC, HCP, BCC, ICOS, OTHER} = output codes 0..5
explicit cutoff argument (distance units); must not exceed pairwise cutoff (error at 74)
CNA signature classification (12 x 421 -> fcc, 6x421+6x422 -> hcp, 6x444+8x666 -> bcc...)

operation.notes: Cutoff choice is the whole game: between 1st/2nd neighbor shell for fcc, larger for bcc.

maps_to.notes: Note-only, deliberately unmapped: a categorical diagnostic label, not a physical quantity; unlikely to ever be a map node. Recorded because phase identification gates which transport model applies.

conventions: Cutoff in distance units, unit-style dependent; results meaningless for liquids (mostly 5=other).

entries.name: thermo-temp
entries.quantity: Global instantaneous kinetic temperature (thermo keyword 'temp' via the auto-created thermo_temp compute)
entries.units: temperature: K (metal/real), reduced epsilon/kB (lj, kB=1)

artifact.name: log.lammps thermo column 'Temp'
artifact.format_notes: Thermo table: header line of column names then rows every 'thermo N' steps; also YAML thermo style available.

source_anchors.what: dof = dimension*N - extra_dof - fix_dof; tfactor = mvv2e/(dof*boltz)
extra_dof default = domain->dimension (COM momentum removal convention)
compute_scalar: T = tfactor * sum m v^2
thermo 'temp' field reads temperature->scalar

operation.closed_form: T = mvv2e * sum_i m_i v_i^2 / ((d N - d - fix_dof) kB)
operation.notes: The T consumed by the GK prefactor and by thermostat setpoints.

maps_to.notes: Grounds the existing Temperature node as an MD OUTPUT (the map currently treats T mainly as an input parameter of thermal_properties/kappa). DOF convention (minus d for COM) is the cross-code comparison hazard at small N.

conventions: dof = d*N - d by default (COM subtraction); fixes register additional dof removal (SHAKE etc.).
Thermostats each carry their OWN temperature compute; thermo 'temp' may differ from the thermostat's T for group/bias reasons.
kB is unit-style dependent (boltz: 8.617343e-5 eV/K metal, 0.0019872067 kcal/mol/K real, 1.0 lj).

entries.name: thermo-energies
entries.quantity: Global energies on stdout: pe (potential), ke = 0.5*dof*kB*T, etotal = pe+ke, enthalpy = etotal + P*V/nktv2p
entries.units: energy - BUT normalized PER ATOM by default in lj units, extensive in real/metal (thermo_modify norm overrides either way)
entries.program: thermo_style custom pe ke etotal enthalpy

artifact.name: log.lammps thermo columns PotEng/KinEng/TotEng/Enthalpy
artifact.format_notes: Column set from thermo_style; 'one' default style prints Step Temp E_pair E_mol TotEng Press.

source_anchors.what: compute_ke: 0.5 * dof * boltz * T - derived from the temperature compute, NOT an independent sum(mv^2/2)
compute_enthalpy: etotal + press*vol/nktv2p
norm default: per-atom normalization ON iff unit style is lj (normvalue=1), OFF otherwise
keyword catalog: press, etotal, enthalpy definitions

operation.closed_form: ke = (dof/2) kB T; H = E + PV (PV converted from pressure*volume to energy via 1/nktv2p)
operation.notes: pe comes from the auto-created thermo_pe compute summing pair+bond+...+kspace+fix energies.

maps_to.notes: Candidate MD-tier nodes (TotalEnergy/Enthalpy of the MD cell). Do NOT map to the catalog's InternalEnergy/Entropy/HelmholtzFreeEnergy - those are per-phonon-mode harmonic quantities with different definitions and dimensions.

conventions: LJ-NORM TRAP: with units lj, pe/ke/etotal/enthalpy print PER ATOM by default (thermo.cpp:196); real/metal print extensive totals. thermo_modify norm yes/no overrides. Cross-unit-style comparisons must undo this.
ke is dof-consistent with 'temp' (0.5*dof*kB*T), so ke != sum 0.5 m v^2 exactly (differs by the COM-dof fraction).
enthalpy uses the current thermo pressure, inheriting its kinetic-term conventions.
example_instances: lammps/examples/KAPPA/in.mp:59 (thermo_style custom step temp epair etotal f_3 v_tdiff)

entries.name: thermo-vol
entries.quantity: Simulation-cell volume (thermo keyword 'vol') - the V of the GK prefactor and of pressure/flux normalizations
entries.units: volume: Angstrom^3 (metal/real), sigma^3 (lj); in 2d, area
entries.program: thermo_style custom vol (also the 'vol' equal-style variable keyword)

artifact.name: log.lammps thermo column 'Volume'
artifact.format_notes: Available in-script as the thermo keyword vol inside equal-style variables - the canonical GK script uses it directly in the scale variable.

source_anchors.what: compute_vol: box volume (xprd*yprd*zprd; area in 2d)
vol consumed in the GK scale variable $s*dt/$t/$t/vol

operation.closed_form: V = lx*ly*lz (orthogonal; triclinic uses the h-matrix determinant)

maps_to.notes: Grounds the existing CellVolume parameter node from the MD side. Under npt it becomes a fluctuating observable rather than a fixed parameter - worth a note on the node.

conventions: Time-dependent under npt/nph/box deform; GK and NEMD normalizations assume the fixed nve/nvt volume.
example_instances: lammps/examples/KAPPA/in.mp:70 (lx*ly and lz keywords, same family)

open_questions: Spatial index on Temperature: is T(chunk/bin) an indexed form of the existing Temperature node or a new ProfileTemperature node? The NEMD gradient edge needs one of the two to be declared (currently the gradient is only an operator parameter).

Reservoir-energy node: fix thermal/conductivity, fix heat, fix ehex and fix langevin tally all realize 'heat pushed through the system' (measured or imposed). Proposed single candidate node ExchangedReservoirEnergy with a scheme for imposed-vs-measured - needs a substrate decision before the NEMD edge can consume it formally.

Should HeatCurrent's node definition record the extensive J*V vs intensive J distinction as a normalization in normalizations.py? LAMMPS emits J*V; GPUMD and most papers quote J or the compacted kappa directly - this is a guaranteed cross-code factor-V trap.

compute heat/flux with stress/atom vs centroid/stress/atom give DIFFERENT J(t) for bonded/many-body systems (only the centroid one is physical for angles/dihedrals) - is that a scheme on compute_heat_current (heat_flux_decomposition) or two different edges? For pair potentials they coincide.

Single-time-origin MSD/VACF (LAMMPS) vs time-origin-averaged definitions (catalog nodes): declare as an estimator/normalization difference on the node, or treat LAMMPS output as a different (windowless) space that only converges to the node's quantity? Affects compare() tolerance policy.

The existing LAMMPS_CONTRACT_KAPPA_NEMD parameter_units hard-code real units (kcal/(mol*fs), K/Angstrom); adapters probably need per-unit-style parameter unit tables or a unit_style scheme on the LAMMPS representation as a whole.

examples/KAPPA README recipes divide the Muller-Plathe gradient over lz/2 but measure dT between bin 1 and bin 11 of 20 - the bin-center offset (half-bin shift) is ignored; is that worth recording as a discretization choice on the NEMD edge?

fix ave/correlate 'ave running' + 'overwrite' is the memory-efficient canonical GK pattern; the windowed 'ave one' estimator has different variance properties. Should correlation_window be a diagnostic discretization choice on autocorrelate_heat_current?

USER-PHONON (fix phonon) and compute smd/tlsph variants were out of scope per the existing adapter docstring; the TALLY heat-flux computes are cataloged note-only - confirm they should stay out of the representation layer.

LAMMPS 'units lj' thermo normalization (per-atom by default) applies to thermo output only, not to compute outputs (c_flux etc. stay extensive) - worth an explicit convention flag on every stdout-artifact entry if the map ever ingests log files automatically.
